intro: The Argentine rental market operates under specific regulations (DNU 70/2023), BCRA inflation indices for annual adjustments, real estate commissions, and recurring expenses like [utilities](/calculadora-expensas-departamento-calcular-m2-categoria). Rental guarantee insurance provides an alternative to property owner guarantees. Values are current for 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you updated.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **% of monthly rent + fees**. Direct calculation from your inputs. Quick estimate for rental guarantee insurance as an alternative to owner guarantees.

explanation:
The **rental market** in Argentina is governed by the Civil and Commercial Code plus DNU 70/2023 (which partially repealed Law 27.551). There is freedom of contract regarding currency, term, and inflation adjustment indices.

## How it's calculated

The cost of rental guarantee insurance to protect rent payments, offered as an alternative to traditional property owner guarantees. Quick and free to calculate.

This calculator applies the formula to your inputs, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside reasonable bounds.

## Important notes

This calculation is an informational reference only. For critical decisions (financial, legal, real estate), consult with a specialized professional. Values are current for 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.a: An insurance policy that replaces traditional security deposits. The insurer covers the landlord if the tenant fails to pay rent.

faq.q: How much does rental guarantee insurance cost?
faq.a: Typically 10–20% of the monthly rent (paid upfront as a one-time premium).
Tenants must generally provide proof of income, employment stability, and clean rental history with no outstanding debts.

faq.q: Is rental guarantee insurance widely accepted?
faq.a: Increasingly, landlords accept guarantee insurance as a valid alternative to traditional security deposits.
No, it must be renewed each time the lease is renewed or extended.

faq.q: What guarantees can a landlord require?
faq.a: Common options include owner guarantees (co-signer with property), guarantee insurance, salary verification (typically 3x monthly rent), or solidarity bonds.

faq.q: Can I cancel rental guarantee insurance early?
faq.a: Yes, but typically with penalties: approximately one month's rent if cancelled in year one, and 1.5 months if after. Most jurisdictions require 3 months' notice to end a lease.

faq.q: Are there limits on rent increases?
faq.a: Under DNU 70/2023: parties can negotiate freely. Otherwise, increases typically follow the national inflation index (BCRA) on an annual basis and are negotiable at lease signing.
